legacy.notes: Default cold-and-dark baseline on the ground.

g950.notes: Default cold-and-dark baseline on the ground.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: Aileron trim runaway — abnormal roll forces. Reduce speed, operate trim switch in correct direction. Pull AIL TRIM ACT CB when force relieved.

after.notes: AIL TRIM ACT CB pulled. Aircraft controllable with residual trim. Land as soon as practicable.

before.notes: One engine shutdown in flight — starting sequence being set up. Aircraft on single engine.

during.notes: Start in progress — starter engaged, NG accelerating, fuel introduced at ~12% NG, T5 rising.

after.notes: Engine stabilised — NG/NP/T5 normal, oil pressure rising. Advance power as required.

before.notes: Before Clearing An Engine: engines running on the ground.

during.notes: During Clearing An Engine: engines running on the ground.

after.notes: After Clearing An Engine: shutdown complete, cold and dark on the ground.

BOTH BOOST PUMP FAILURE SAME TANK.title: Both Boost Pumps Failed — Same Tank

before.notes: Normal cruise, both tanks supplying respective engines normally.

during.notes: AFT BOOST1 PR AND AFT BOOST2 PR annunciated — both AFT tank pumps failed. QRH: STBY BOOST PUMP EMER (AFT) ON; pull No.1 CB; if BOOST2 PR stays on: FUEL SELECTOR CROSSFEED FROM FWD TANK.

after.notes: Crossfeed established. Both engines drawing from FWD tank. Monitor quantities. Land as soon as practical.

FAILURE TO ACCELERATE HUNG START OR HIGH.title: Failure to Accelerate / Hung Start / High T5

before.notes: Start in progress — fuel introduced, NG should be accelerating.

during.notes: Hung start: NG stalled / not accelerating normally. OR High T5: temperature rising above start limit. QRH: abort start — fuel lever CUTOFF, continue motoring to purge.

after.notes: Start aborted. Engine motoring. Cooling down. Identify cause before reattempt.

before.notes: Before Flapless Landing: approach setup, airborne.

during.notes: During Flapless Landing: landing configuration, airborne.

after.notes: After Flapless Landing: rollout complete on the ground.

before.notes: Normal cruise, adequate fuel both sides.

during.notes: AFT FUEL LOW CAS — ~200 lb in AFT collector cell. Monitor quantity. If fuel transfer failure suspected: earlier indication (300–500 lb). QRH fuel low level procedure.

after.notes: Diversion commenced. Monitoring fuel quantities on both sides. Land as soon as practical.

before.notes: Normal cruise, fuel transferring normally.

during.notes: Fuel transfer failure — ejector/level-control valve blocked. FUEL LOW LEVEL illuminates early (~330 lb). All 4 cells declining equally. QRH fuel transfer failure procedure.

after.notes: Crossfeed selected if required. Landing as soon as practical.

before.notes: Normal cruise, both generators on-line.

during.notes: GEN L CAS — single generator failure. Bus tie connecting both buses to right generator. QRH: check CB, attempt reset once. Shed non-essential loads.

after.notes: Single generator ops. Loads shed. VDC stable. Plan approach.

before.notes: Engine started, NG above 70%, expecting generator to come on-line.

during.notes: L GEN light does not illuminate after start. QRH: check GEN switch ON, check GEN CB, attempt reset.

after.notes: Generator reset or operating on right generator only. Investigate before further flight.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: Power lever entered beta/reverse in flight. Severe drag. Advance power lever to forward immediately.

after.notes: Power lever forward, aircraft recovering. Land as soon as possible.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: Power lever entered beta/reverse in flight — PROHIBITED. Severe drag. QRH: advance power lever to forward flight range immediately. With blade latches: prop may have latched at +1°.

after.notes: Power lever returned forward. Aircraft recovering. Land as soon as possible. Engineering inspection required.

before.notes: Normal cruise, hydraulic pressure 1450 psi.

during.notes: HYD LO PRESS CAS — pressure below 1225 psi. QRH: hand pump 30–40 strokes to 1500 psi. If rises: pump failed — check CB (do NOT reset in flight). If no rise after 40 strokes: fluid loss.

after.notes: Pump failure confirmed. CB pulled. Hand pump for flap ops. Maintain 1500 psi with flaps extended. Do not retract flaps until stopped.

before.notes: Start initiated — NG accelerating, fuel lever moved to RUN at ~12% NG.

during.notes: No T5 rise after fuel lever RUN. No combustion after 10–15 sec. QRH: abort — fuel lever CUTOFF, continue motoring to purge. Check fuel supply, ignition.

after.notes: Start aborted. Motoring to purge. Investigate: fuel ON? Ignition serviceable? Correct sequence?

before.notes: Normal cruise, oil pressure 90 psi.

during.notes: L OIL PRESS CAS — pressure 45 psi (caution range 40–85 psi). Reduce power. If below 40 psi: engine shutdown required.

after.notes: Power reduced. Oil pressure monitored. If drops below 40 psi: engine shutdown. Land as soon as practical.

before.notes: Normal cruise, oil pressure 90 psi.

during.notes: L OIL PRESS CAS — pressure 45 psi (caution range 40–85 psi). Reduce power. If below 40 psi: engine shutdown required.

after.notes: Power reduced. Oil pressure monitored. If drops below 40 psi: engine shutdown. Land as soon as practical.

before.notes: Normal cruise, oil 90 psi.

during.notes: L oil pressure 55 psi — 40–80 psi caution range. Reduce power, monitor. 40–80 psi acceptable for ground ops only. Report as engine discrepancy.

after.notes: Power reduced. Pressure monitored. Landing planned.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: L NG HIGH CAS — NG exceeding 101.5%. QRH: reduce power lever until NG within limits. 2-sec limit: 102.6%.

after.notes: Power reduced. NG back within limits. Monitor all parameters. If uncontrollable: engine shutdown.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: NP exceeding 101.5% (CSU/overspeed governor failure). QRH: MAX power unaffected engine, shutdown affected engine per engine shutdown in flight checklist.

after.notes: Affected engine shut down, prop feathered. Single engine approach. Land as soon as possible.

before.notes: Normal cruise, NP at selected governor setting.

during.notes: NP above prop lever selection but below 101.5% — partial governor malfunction. QRH: land as soon as practical. Problem must be corrected before further flight.

after.notes: Monitoring. Landing at nearest suitable airport.

before.notes: Normal cruise, T5 within limits.

during.notes: L T5 HIGH CAS — T5 exceeding 725°C (PT6A-27) / 790°C (PT6A-34). QRH: reduce NG. If cannot maintain within limits: engine shutdown in flight.

after.notes: Power reduced, T5 within limits. Monitor. If uncontrollable: engine shutdown in flight.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: CB has tripped (popped). Identify affected system. Do NOT reset in flight except for safety of flight — and only once. If electrical fire suspected: do not reset.

after.notes: If reset attempted (once only) and held: monitor. If tripped again: isolate and land.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: Left prop has feathered without crew command — NP and torque dropping, NG still running. QRH: MAX power right engine; verify prop lever not at FEATHER; if autofeather ON: turn OFF.

after.notes: If prop does not unfeather: execute engine shutdown in flight, land as soon as practical.

before.notes: Before Both Engines Inoperative Landing: approach setup, airborne.

during.notes: During Both Engines Inoperative Landing: landing configuration, airborne.

after.notes: After Both Engines Inoperative Landing: rollout complete on the ground.

BOTH GENERATOR FAIL CAS MESSAGE ANNUNCIA.title: Both Generators Failed / Total Electrical Failure

before.notes: Normal cruise, both generators on-line, VDC 28V.

during.notes: Both GEN lights on (or VDC dropping). Battery is now sole DC source. QRH: attempt reset both generators. If unsuccessful: shed all non-essential loads, declare emergency, land immediately.

after.notes: Generators unreset. Battery-only power. Declare emergency. Land immediately — avionics life limited.

EMERGENCY DESCENT HIGH SPEED ZERO FLAPS.title: Emergency Descent High Speed Zero Flaps

before.notes: Before Emergency Descent High Speed Zero Flaps: stable cruise, airborne.

during.notes: During Emergency Descent High Speed Zero Flaps: descent profile, airborne.

after.notes: After Emergency Descent High Speed Zero Flaps: approach setup, airborne.

EMERGENCY DESCENT LOW SPEED FULL FLAPS.title: Emergency Descent Low Speed Full Flaps

before.notes: Before Emergency Descent Low Speed Full Flaps: stable cruise, airborne.

during.notes: During Emergency Descent Low Speed Full Flaps: descent profile, airborne.

after.notes: After Emergency Descent Low Speed Full Flaps: approach setup, airborne.

before.notes: Before Emergency Landing Checklist: approach setup, airborne.

during.notes: During Emergency Landing Checklist: landing configuration, airborne.

after.notes: After Emergency Landing Checklist: rollout complete on the ground.

ENGINE FAILURE AFTER AIRBORNE PRIOR TO V.title: Engine Failure After Airborne Prior To V1

before.notes: Before Engine Failure After Airborne Prior To V1: initial climb after liftoff.

during.notes: During Engine Failure After Airborne Prior To V1: affected engine secured, airborne.

after.notes: After Engine Failure After Airborne Prior To V1: single-engine airborne state maintained.

before.notes: Before Engine Failure Prior To Airborne: line-up / takeoff-ready ground state.

during.notes: During Engine Failure Prior To Airborne: line-up / takeoff-ready ground state.

after.notes: After Engine Failure Prior To Airborne: taxi configuration on the ground.

ENGINE FIRE FAILURE AFTER TAKEOFF AFTER.title: Engine Fire Failure After Takeoff After V1

before.notes: Before Engine Fire Failure After Takeoff After V1: initial climb after liftoff.

during.notes: During Engine Fire Failure After Takeoff After V1: climb power maintained, airborne.

after.notes: After Engine Fire Failure After Takeoff After V1: stable cruise, airborne.

ENGINE FIRE FAILURE IN FLIGHT OR INFLIGH.title: Engine Fire Failure In Flight Or Inflight Shutdown

before.notes: Before Engine Fire Failure In Flight Or Inflight Shutdown: engines running on the ground.

during.notes: During Engine Fire Failure In Flight Or Inflight Shutdown: shutdown in progress on the ground.

after.notes: After Engine Fire Failure In Flight Or Inflight Shutdown: engines available for the next phase.

before.notes: Before Engine Fire On Ground: engines running on the ground.

during.notes: During Engine Fire On Ground: engines running on the ground.

after.notes: After Engine Fire On Ground: shutdown complete, cold and dark on the ground.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: Smoke or fire in cockpit/cabin. QRH: identify source if possible. If electrical — DC MASTER sequence. If unknown — unknown source QRH. Oxygen on, descend, land immediately.

after.notes: Oxygen on, descend executed, source isolated/controlled. Emergency declared, landing in progress.

before.notes: Before RTO: take-off roll in progress, both engines at take-off power, accelerating.

during.notes: During RTO: abort called. Power levers IDLE, maximum braking. Props MAX or FEATHER if fire. Announce STOPPING.

after.notes: After RTO: aircraft stopped, brakes held. Emergency services alerted. Shut down if fire or damage suspected.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: Smoke or smell of burning insulation — suspected electrical fire. QRH: DC MASTER OFF then ON to identify bus. Shed non-essential loads. Oxygen on. Land immediately.

after.notes: DC MASTER restored, source isolated, loads shed. Emergency declared, landing in progress.

before.notes: Normal cruise, both generators on-line.

during.notes: Total electrical failure — VDC decaying, no generators. Battery only. QRH: attempt reset both generators. If unsuccessful: land immediately, avionics life limited.

after.notes: Both generators unreset. Battery powering minimal systems. Emergency declared. Land immediately.

before.notes: Before After Landing Checks: aircraft on late final / rollout state before the after-landing flow is started.

during.notes: During After Landing Checks: rollout initiated and waiting for touchdown while after-landing actions are being completed.

after.notes: After After Landing Checks: checks complete, aircraft on the ground and configured for taxi to the holding bay.

before.notes: Before After Start Checks: all startup lights on, master caution on, props feathered, both engines not started.

during.notes: During After Start Checks: one engine started on the ground, started-side prop full forward, both generator lights still on, reduced caution set remains.

after.notes: After After Start Checks: both engines started, no caution lights, pre-taxi ground state.

before.notes: Before After Take-Off Checks: initial climb after liftoff.

during.notes: During After Take-Off Checks: climb power maintained, airborne.

after.notes: After After Take-Off Checks: stable cruise, airborne.

before.notes: Before Climb: just airborne, transitioning from take-off power to climb.

during.notes: During Climb Power setting: flaps up, gear up, climb power set (NG ~90%, T5 within climb limit).

after.notes: After Climb Power: established in climb, all instruments in limits. Monitoring.

before.notes: Transitioning from climb — reducing to cruise power.

during.notes: Cruise power set. NG, T5, torque stabilising. All engine instrument cross-checks.

after.notes: Cruise established. All parameters checked and normal.

before.notes: Before Descent Checks: stable cruise, airborne.

during.notes: During Descent Checks: descent profile, airborne.

after.notes: After Descent Checks: approach setup, airborne.

before.notes: Before Go-Around: approach setup, airborne.

during.notes: During Go-Around: go-around power set, airborne.

after.notes: After Go-Around: stable climb / cruise state.

before.notes: Before Landing: approach setup, airborne.

during.notes: During Landing: landing configuration, airborne.

after.notes: After Landing: rollout complete on the ground.

before.notes: Before Line-Up: holding short of runway, awaiting ATC clearance.

during.notes: During Line-Up: entering runway. Final items — transponder to ALT, landing lights ON, clock started.

after.notes: After Line-Up: lined up on runway centreline. Ready to advance power.

before.notes: Before Pre-Start Checklist: cold and dark on the ground.

during.notes: During Pre-Start Checklist: ground state retained for training setup.

after.notes: After Pre-Start Checklist: engines running on the ground.

before.notes: Before Safety Inspection Checks: cold and dark on the ground.

during.notes: During Safety Inspection Checks: ground state retained for training setup.

after.notes: After Safety Inspection Checks: engines running on the ground.

before.notes: Before Shutdown Checks: engines running on the ground.

during.notes: During Shutdown Checks: shutdown in progress on the ground.

after.notes: After Shutdown Checks: engines available for the next phase.

before.notes: Before Taxi: engines started, before-taxi checks beginning.

during.notes: During Taxi Checks: flight controls checked for full free movement, instruments checked, altimeters set, fuel selectors NORM.

after.notes: After Taxi Checks: all items complete. Ready to taxi.

before.notes: Normal cruise, G950 AHRS serviceable.

during.notes: Attitude/heading mismatch between AHRS sources on G950. QRH: select alternate AHRS. Cross-check standby AI.

after.notes: Alternate AHRS selected and verified. Displays consistent.

before.notes: Normal cruise, pitot-static serviceable.

during.notes: ASI/altimeter/VSI erroneous or frozen. QRH: pitot heat ON, select alternate static if available, use standby instruments.

after.notes: Standby instruments in use. Pitot heat verified ON. Report for maintenance.

before.notes: Normal cruise, all avionics serviceable.

during.notes: EFD 1000 dark or failure flags. QRH: check circuit breaker. Use G950 PFD as primary. Cross-check standby instruments.

after.notes: Operating on G950 PFD and standby instruments. EFD failure isolated. Land at suitable airfield.

before.notes: Normal cruise, comms serviceable.

during.notes: Audio panel failure — intercom or radio audio lost. QRH: check CB, attempt direct radio bypass. Squawk 7600 if unable to re-establish comms.

after.notes: Alternate audio path established or failure isolated. Continue to nearest suitable airport.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: CHIP DET caution light on. Magnetic chip in engine gearbox oil — potential impending failure. Monitor oil pressure and temp closely. QRH: reduce power, land as soon as practical.

after.notes: Power reduced, monitoring. Landed. Engineering inspection required before further flight.

before.notes: Normal cruise, radio comms serviceable.

during.notes: Unable to contact ATC on any frequency. QRH: squawk 7600. Continue to destination per lost comms procedures. Attempt all available means.

after.notes: Squawking 7600, following lost comms procedure. Inform ATC by all means on landing.

before.notes: Normal cruise, all instruments serviceable.

during.notes: Altitude or heading indicator erroneous. Cross-check standby instruments. QRH: identify and isolate failed instrument.

after.notes: Failed instrument isolated. Using standby instruments for confirmed data.

before.notes: Normal cruise, G600 serviceable.

during.notes: G600 AHRS/ADC failure flags. QRH: select alternate AHRS/ADC source. Cross-check standby instruments.

after.notes: Alternate source selected and verified. Displays consistent.

before.notes: Normal cruise, MFD serviceable.

during.notes: G600 MFD dark or failure flags. QRH: check CB. Use PFD as primary. Engine data from backup gauges.

after.notes: PFD as primary. Continuing to nearest suitable airport.

before.notes: Normal IFR cruise, GPS active.

during.notes: GPS sensor fail message. Navigation data invalid. Revert to VOR/ADF/DR. Inform ATC.

after.notes: Alternate navigation established. ATC informed of GPS degradation.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: G600 monitor caution. Identify affected system. Cross-check alternate sources.

after.notes: Affected system identified and operating within limits or isolated.

before.notes: Normal cruise, dual GPS active.

during.notes: One GPS sensor failed — system reverted to single GPS. Navigation data valid but limited. Inform ATC if required.

after.notes: Single GPS operation. Limitation noted. Report for maintenance.

before.notes: Normal cruise, all heading sources consistent.

during.notes: Heading disagreement between sources. Identify correct source using GPS track or visual reference. Isolate failed indicator.

after.notes: Correct heading source selected and verified. Continuing flight.

before.notes: Normal cruise, GPS/SBAS active.

during.notes: GPS/SBAS data loss. Approach capability degraded. QRH: revert to ILS/VOR. Inform ATC of navigation change required.

after.notes: ILS/VOR approach planned. ATC informed.

before.notes: Normal cruise, navigation equipment serviceable.

during.notes: Navigation system failure. Identify failed component. Revert to backup nav method. Inform ATC.

after.notes: Backup navigation active. Continuing with available navigation references.

before.notes: Normal cruise.

during.notes: PITOT HEAT caution — heater failed or CB tripped. Check CB. Monitor ASI for icing signs. Avoid icing conditions.

after.notes: CB checked. If icing: avoid and land as soon as practical.

before.notes: Normal cruise, instruments reading correctly.

during.notes: ASI/altimeter/VSI erroneous or frozen. Pitot heat ON. Use standby instruments. Consider alternate static if fitted.

after.notes: Standby instruments in use. Pitot heat verified on. Report for maintenance.

before.notes: Approaching take-off, prop levers inadvertently not at MAX.

during.notes: RESET PROPS CAS — prop levers not at MAX RPM. DO NOT advance power levers. QRH: advance both prop levers to MAX RPM.

after.notes: Both props at MAX RPM. RESET PROPS extinguished. Cleared to continue.

before.notes: Normal cruise, radio comms serviceable.

during.notes: Unable to contact ATC on any frequency. QRH: squawk 7600, continue to destination, fly standard lost comms procedures.

after.notes: Squawking 7600. Following lost comms procedure. Advise ATC on landing by all available means.

before.notes: Before Take-Off Checks: taxiing to holding point, pre-taxi checks done, approaching holding point.

during.notes: During Take-Off Checks: autofeather armed, flaps selected, trim in green, props MAX RPM, crossfeed NORM.

after.notes: After Take-Off Checks: all items complete. RESET PROPS extinguished, autofeather ARM confirmed. Ready for line-up.

before.notes: Before Engine Fire: normal cruise, airborne, both engines running. Autofeather OFF (approved take-off only).

during.notes: During Engine Fire: left engine FIRE warning annunciated. Left engine is on fire — QRH memory items sequence: max power (right), confirm/retard left power lever, feather left prop, fuel lever L OFF, fuel emergency L OFF, FIRE PULL L, discharge extinguisher, boost pump L OFF.

after.notes: After Engine Fire: left engine shut down and secured. Single engine cruise. Fire warning should extinguish once heat source removed. Land as soon as possible.

before.notes: Before OEI: initial climb after take-off, both engines producing take-off power. Both props MAX. Autofeather armed for take-off.

during.notes: During OEI: left engine has failed (low NG, torque 0, fuel flow 0, prop windmilling or autofeathered). Right engine at max. Maintain VYSE (82 KCAS). Memory items: max power right, identify (dead foot = dead engine), confirm prop FEATHER left.

after.notes: After OEI: left engine feathered, secured. Single engine climb at VYSE 82 KCAS. Continue QRH engine failure items then engine shutdown in flight checklist.

before.notes: Before Low Oil Pressure: normal cruise, oil pressure in normal range (85–105 psi).

during.notes: During Low Oil Pressure: left engine OIL PRESS L caution. Oil pressure 45 psi — in caution range (40–85 psi). Reduce power, monitor. If below 40 psi: engine shutdown required.

after.notes: After Low Oil Pressure: power reduced, monitoring. Plan to land as soon as practicable. If oil pressure drops below 40 psi at any time: engine shutdown in flight is required — oil below 40 psi is unsafe.

before.notes: Before High T5: normal cruise, T5 within limits (max cruise 740°C recommended).

during.notes: During High T5: left engine T5 rising above limit (795°C, above 790°C take-off max). QRH: reduce NG (retard power lever) until T5 within limits. If cannot maintain within limits: engine shutdown in flight.

after.notes: After High T5: power lever reduced to bring T5 below limit. T5 now 755°C, within limits. If T5 could not be controlled: engine would be shut down.

before.notes: Before Propeller Overspeed: normal cruise, NP controlled at 96% (MAX).

during.notes: During Propeller Overspeed: left NP has exceeded 101.5% (governing failure). QRH: power and prop levers unaffected engine MAX power; shutdown affected engine per engine shutdown in flight checklist.

after.notes: After Propeller Overspeed: affected engine shut down, prop feathered. Land as soon as possible — overspeed may indicate CSU failure; engine condition unknown.

before.notes: Before Uncommanded Feather: normal cruise, both props governing at MAX RPM.

during.notes: During Uncommanded Feather: left prop has feathered without crew command — NP and torque dropping to zero, NG still running. QRH: confirm left prop lever not selected FEATHER; check autofeather switch. Power and prop levers unaffected engine MAX. If autofeather system is ON: turn OFF.

after.notes: After Uncommanded Feather: if propeller does not unfeather, carry out engine shutdown in flight and land as soon as practical. If unfeathered and normal, continue with caution.

before.notes: Before NG Overspeed: normal cruise, NG at 75%.

during.notes: During NG Overspeed: left engine NG has exceeded 101.5%. QRH: reduce power lever until NG within limits. If NG cannot be maintained within limits: engine shutdown in flight.

after.notes: After NG Overspeed: power lever retarded, NG reduced to within limits (101.5% max). Monitor for T5, torque, and oil pressure anomalies.

before.notes: Before Both Gen Fail: normal cruise, both generators online, 28 VDC.

during.notes: During Both Gen Fail: both generator lights on, bus tie relay has connected buses. Battery is now sole power source. Load shed immediately: QRH both generators illuminated — attempt reset. If no reset: land immediately. G950: avionics emergency lighting powered from battery. Battery endurance limited.

after.notes: After Both Gen Fail: if reset unsuccessful, on battery power only. Shed non-essential loads. Land as soon as possible. VDC will decay as battery discharges.
